description:
The 'install' command currently wipes state.json on completion, losing all previously managed configs and dependencies. Additionally, the dashboard masks granular sync failures, reporting success even when individual configs fail to stow.

Plan:
1. Fix State Corruption: Update setup.SaveState in internal/setup/setup.go to use state.Load() instead of state.New(), ensuring existing state is merged with the new InstallResult.
2. Improve Sync Transparency: Update handleAction in cmd/g4d/interactive.go to inspect StowResult.Failed for ActionSync, ActionSyncConfig, and ActionBulkSync, reporting specific failures to the user.
3. Unify Dashboard Status: Refactor internal/ui/dashboard/sidebar.go to unify getConfigStatusInfo, prioritizing granular LinkStatus over count-based DriftSummary.
4. Verification: Add unit tests in internal/setup/setup_test.go to verify state merging.
title: Fix critical state corruption and sync transparency bugs

This gives unique value by helping users understand the relationship between source and destination. File: internal/ui/dashboard/panels/details_panel.go
notes: Related to go4dot-ov1.5 (drift visualization) - tree view should show drift status inline
title: Improve details view with tree hierarchy

description:
Two VHS tape files exist without corresponding test functions in vhs_visual_test.go:
- cli_help.tape - tests CLI help output
- onboarding_flow.tape - tests onboarding wizard flow

These tapes can be run manually but aren't part of the automated test suite (make e2e-visual).

Options:
1. Add TestVHS_CliHelp and TestVHS_OnboardingFlow functions
2. Remove the tapes if they're not needed
3. Document them as manual-only tests
notes: Resolution: Added TestVHS_OnboardingFlow to vhs_visual_test.go. Note: cli_help.tape already had TestCLIHelp in cli_test.go - only onboarding_flow.tape was actually orphaned. Golden file will be created on first run with UPDATE_GOLDEN=1.
title: Add tests for orphaned VHS tapes or remove them

notes: IMPLEMENTATION APPROACH: Docker-first strategy - skipping local system testing (risky) and jumping directly to containerized E2E tests. Tests will run inside Docker containers using existing test/docker/ infrastructure, eliminating all safety concerns. VHS tapes and teatest scenarios execute in isolated container environments. No need for Phase 4 orchestrator initially - standard 'go test' with Docker helpers is sufficient.
title: Comprehensive E2E Test Scenarios

description: Support using ONE dotfiles repo across MULTIPLE machines with different configurations. Real use case: Fedora KDE laptop, CachyOS laptop, macOS - all sharing same dotfiles repo but needing machine-specific local config. Current .go4dot.yaml only works for one machine setup. Need: 1) Machine/host detection and identification. 2) Per-machine config overrides (which configs to install, machine-specific values). 3) Conditional dependencies per platform/machine. 4) Single .go4dot.yaml that works everywhere OR machine-specific overlay files. Should feel seamless - run g4d install on any machine and it does the right thing.
title: Multi-machine dotfiles support

close_reason: Core new-machine setup flow landed in PRs #104-112: SSH key detection/generation (#104), GitHub key registration (#105), smart prompt defaults (#106), setup verification (#107), MachineView rendering (#109), git-signing preset (#110), auto-detect prompt (#111), post-configure verify (#112)
description: When setting up a new computer, there's a bunch of annoying manual steps beyond just symlinking dotfiles: generating SSH keys, generating GPG keys, configuring git with signing, setting up credential helpers, etc. go4dot could be the ONE tool that handles all this tedium. Features: 1) SSH key generation with sensible defaults. 2) GPG key generation and git signing setup. 3) Add SSH key to GitHub/GitLab. 4) Configure git user.name/email. 5) Other common new-machine setup tasks. This makes g4d install truly a one-stop shop for new machine setup - unique value proposition.
title: New machine setup wizard (SSH, GPG, common tedium)

This ties into the file tree improvements - users should see at a glance what's in sync and what's drifted.
title: Enhanced source/dest drift visualization and detection

description:
## Problem Statement

The dashboard UI currently has several UX issues that break the unified experience:

### Issues Identified

1. **Tab menu not rendering** - When pressing tab, the menu view appears empty. The menu is created with 0 dimensions and WindowSizeMsg isn't forwarded properly when switching to viewMenu.

2. **Operations take over entire screen** - `viewOperation` completely replaces the dashboard instead of showing operations in a panel/split. Users lose context of what they were looking at.

3. **Many actions fall back to CLI output outside dashboard:**
   - `ActionDoctor` → `doctorCmd.Run()` → CLI output + `waitForEnter()`
   - `ActionMachineConfig` → `machine.RunInteractiveConfig()` → huh forms
   - `ActionList` → `runMoreMenu()` → huh forms
   - External menu → huh forms
   - Uninstall → huh forms with CLI progress

4. **Log visibility limited** - Only last 10 logs shown (`operations.go:211`), no scrolling, logs get lost during long operations.

5. **No split-pane architecture** - Currently only header/summary/sidebar/details/footer. No dedicated output/log pane.

### User Request

Make the UI more like lazygit - dynamic, resizable, with dense information display that stays contained in one unified interface.

### Potential Solutions to Investigate

1. **Split pane for operations** - Add a third vertical pane (sidebar | details | output) or horizontal split (main content | log output at bottom)

2. **Modal overlay** - Show operations in a modal with dashboard visible behind (semi-transparent or partial)

3. **Inline operation status** - Show operation progress in the details pane or footer area without switching views

4. **Scrollable log viewport** - Use bubbles/viewport component for scrollable log history

5. **Unified component architecture** - Bring doctor, machine config, external, uninstall actions into dashboard components rather than falling back to CLI/huh forms

### Reference

- lazygit uses a consistent panel-based layout where different panes show different aspects (files, commits, branches, log)
- Operations in lazygit happen with live output visible in a pane
- Terminal resizing is handled gracefully

### Files to Review

- `internal/ui/dashboard/dashboard.go` - main model and view switching
- `internal/ui/dashboard/operations.go` - current operation display (full-screen)
- `internal/ui/dashboard/menu.go` - broken tab menu
- `cmd/g4d/interactive.go` - handleAction falls back to CLI for many actions
title: Investigate dashboard UI consolidation and lazygit-style layout
